message:
feat(supervision): replace clock stub with monotonic temporal kernel (bd-18sp)

The supervisor's computed_now_ms() was a fake clock that derived time from
restart_timestamps.len() * 1000, making the sliding-window restart budget
non-temporal and impossible to test under real timing conditions.

Introduce a MonotonicClock trait with two implementations:
- SteadyMonotonicClock: production clock backed by std::time::Instant
- DeterministicMonotonicClock: manually-driven clock for tests/lab replay

The supervisor now uses the real clock for all time-dependent decisions:
- Sliding-window restart-budget pruning uses real monotonic timestamps
- SupervisorHealth reports current_time_ms and oldest_restart_age_ms
- Clock regression is rejected with ERR_SUP_CLOCK_REGRESSION
- Supervisor::with_deterministic_clock() constructor enables lab-style
  schedule replay without non-determinism

Add exhaustive restart-budget reference-model test that checks every
combination of timing deltas against an independent reference
implementation, plus clock regression and serde round-trip tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
